content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission


December 23, 1987

Donald Donaldson
460 Hazelwood Avenue
San Francisco, CA 94127

RE: Your Request for Information
Our File No. I-87-302

Dear Mr. Donaldson:

You have requested information regarding the campaign
provisions of the Political Reform Act of 1974.1/ All your
questions pertain to the use of campaign funds by Mayor Dianne
Feinstein.

The Political Reform Act of 1974, administered and
interpreted by the Fair Political Practices Commission,
requires that all contributions received and expenditures made
by candidates, officeholders, and campaign committees be
disclosed. The Act does not place any restrictions on the use
or expenditure of such funds. However, the California
Elections Code, Section 12400, et seq. does address the use of
campaign funds.

The Elections Code is interpreted by the State Attorney
General's office. You may obtain information by writing to the
Department of Justice, 1515 K Street, Suite 511, Sacramento,
CA 95814.

Sincerely,

Diane M. Griffiths
General Counsel

By: Kevin S. Braaten-Moen
Political Reform Consultant


1/Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise
indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California
Administrative Code Section 18000, et seq. All references to
regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California
Administrative Code.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660

Friday,November 27th,1987
460 Hazelwood Avenue,
San Francisco (941-27),California

[- ? t-

Nov 30 Dated.Nov.30,1987 California Fair Political Practices Commission,
428 "J" Street,
Sacramento (958-149,California

subject: non-candidate Dianne Feinstein

Greetings:

On file in the Registar of Voters office in San Francisco
County is a "Statement of Organization (Recipient Committee" under the
name of Nob Rheims of Mayor Diane Feinstein (1988)". On schedule "H" of
this document are,in my estimation,some questionable items:consider the
fact Dianne Feinstein is not a candidate,at this time,for any political
office,nor has been since being elected Mayor of San Francisco.

Why are "official gifts" part in a non-campaign? It is claimed
payment to a & T Jewelers ($112.30),Sakura Florists
($231.65),Christophers Enterprises ($180.38),Gump's ($2,700.47),Nanley
Hoff Dinner Committee ($150.00),Heirloom Engravers ($462.00),Michael St.
Michel ($770.12),Outsideau & "Lower Service ($262.20),L.M. Erickson Fashion-
ions ($355.28), Thomas John Gibbons-Photography ($482.40),Tie Irish Store
($1,104.20),Tiffany & Company ($5,084.99),Winsor Vineyards,Inc. (1,536.00),
are official gifts. It will be appreciated if you will send me the Commission's legal description of "Official gifts",as used as a part of a political campaign.

Why is"official entertaining" a part of a non-campaign? It is
claimed a "reimbursement"Diannae Feinstein ($1,755.29),Richard O. Blum
(Mrs Dianne Feinstein?) "reimbursement" (692.33), Robert Fredmore Catering ($1,577.46), San Francisco Chamber or Commerce ($300.78),San Francisco
Hilton & Towers ($1,398.10),Say Cheese Caterers ($126.00),St Francis Yacht
Club ($102.78),and World Trade Club $1,112.47) are legal expenditures.

For What is the Commission's legal foundation for allowing the expenses af- the Mayor's Far East Trip (Tokyo) to be paid for from (non)
campaign expenses? Such as Charlotte Mailliard ($1,294.99),Hotel Okura
Tokyo,($3,051.95)($1,832.93),Richard O. Blum(reimbursement ($4,585.91),
Hotels Okura ($1,070.19),Royal (Osaka)($1,056.75),Mandarin (Hong Kong)
($1,852.67), United group Corp. (Osaka)($600.00),Thomas Wastham (reimbursement)($2,105.56),and Travel Arts ($2,732.00).

If the Commission has a list of items legally deductable from
campaign expenditures,for both candidates and non-candidates,it will be
appreciated if you will send me a copy.

If the items listed in the above paragraphs are non-deductable
what legal action(s) does the Commission intend to take for recovery?

c.o. Dianne Blum,AKA Mrs. Richrad Blum, Yours,
AKA Dianne Feinstein,Mayor,S.F.
AKA Queen of Corruption
[signature] Donald Donaldson,non-candidate

December 4, 1987


Donald Donaldson
400 Hazelwood Avenue
San Francisco, CA  94127

                                                Re:  87-302

Dear Mr. Donaldson:

     Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform
Act was received on November 30, 1987 by the Fair Political
Practices Commission.  If you have any questions about your
advice request, you may contact me directly at (916) 322-5662.

     We try to answer all advice requests promptly.  Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions,
or more information is needed, you should expect a response
within 21 working days if your request seeks formal written
advice.  If more information is needed, the person assigned to
prepare a response to your request will contact you shortly to
advise you as to the information needed.  If your request is
for informal assistance, we will answer it as quickly as we
can.  (See Commission Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Adm. Code Sec.
18329).)

     You also should be aware that your letter and our response
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon
receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

                                        Very truly yours,


                                        [illegible]
                                        Jeanne Pritchard
                                        Chief
                                        Technical Assistance and Analysis
                                        Division

428 J Street, Suite 800  •  P.O. Box 807  •  Sacramento CA 95804-0807  •  (916) 322-5660
